user_query:
I need a robust orchestration script to manage a new coding session using the helper scripts in ./scripts/ and settings in ./config.json.

Please do the following:
1) Read input/task_spec.json for: project_name, plan_prompt, build_prompt, monitoring ("standard" or "realtime"), and optional provider/model hints.
2) Create output/orchestrator.py that:
   - Uses only relative paths.
   - Reads ./config.json to get base_url and projects_base_dir.
   - Verifies the local server is healthy by polling base_url + "/global/health" until it reports healthy (retry up to ~15 seconds with a short delay) before proceeding.
   - Updates providers via ./scripts/update_providers.sh.
   - Creates the project directory under projects_base_dir/project_name (do not hardcode any absolute paths).
   - Creates a session using ./scripts/create_session.sh, captures the session ID, and saves state (./scripts/save_state.sh).
   - If provider/model hints are present in task_spec.json, resolve them via ./scripts/select_provider.sh and pass results to ./scripts/send_message.sh; otherwise use defaults.
   - Sends two messages: first a planning message with the plan_prompt using the "plan" agent, then an implementation message with the build_prompt using the "build" agent (or default if agent selection is implicit in the helper scripts).
   - Monitoring behavior depends on monitoring:
     • standard: implement an intelligent polling loop using ./scripts/check_status.sh with exponential backoff (start around 2s, cap around 30s) until the session is idle.
     • realtime: stream progress with ./scripts/monitor_session.sh.
   - When idle, retrieve a diff summary via ./scripts/get_diff.sh and write it to output/diff_summary.txt; write the session ID to output/session_id.txt.
   - Do not read or inspect any project source files directly; rely on the diff summary and helper scripts only.
3) Create output/run_log.txt with a concise, human-readable rundown of the steps the orchestrator will perform in order (e.g., Verify server health → Update providers → Create project → Create session → Send plan prompt → Send build prompt → Monitor until idle → Get diff summary), one step per line.
4) Create output/diff_summary.txt and output/session_id.txt pre-filled with the exact single-line placeholder "NOT_EXECUTED". These will be overwritten when the orchestrator is actually run later.
5) Create output/runbook.md describing how to run output/orchestrator.py, what it does, what files it writes (output/diff_summary.txt and output/session_id.txt), and a brief note about the two monitoring modes.

Important:
- Use only the helper scripts in ./scripts/ for session management, messaging, monitoring, and diff retrieval.
- Do not read or modify any generated code files directly; rely on diff summaries and status endpoints.
- Use only relative workspace paths like input/, output/, ./config.json, and ./scripts/.

Write all deliverables under output/ with the exact paths specified above.
